resources.string: Are you sure you want to uninstall module %s?
Are you sure you want to uninstall module %s? This action will affect all modules, and certain features provided by the metamodule (such as mounting) will no longer work
Jailbreak may have failed, please check logs
Modules are unavailable due to a conflict with Magisk!
KernelSU will remain free and open source; donate to official KernelSU developers to show your support.

View source code at %1$s
Join our %2$s channel
Prevent processes escalated with this profile (and their children) from re-escalating via KernelSU, e.g. running su again or calling GRANT_ROOT. Recommended.
Failed to update App Profile for %s
Persist this app signature and trust it as a dynamic manager
Failed to set %s as dynamic manager
Failed to remove %s from dynamic managers
Unable to extract dynamic manager signature for %s
Allow any app as dynamic manager
Experimental. Show the dynamic manager switch for every app instead of only preset manager signatures.
The current KernelSU version %s is too low for the manager to work properly. Please upgrade to version %s or higher!
The manager (UAPI %1$d) and kernel (UAPI %2$d) are out of sync. Please update both the kernel and the manager to matching versions.
Umount modules for apps without their own profile. Off by default.
Default root profile (manager and shell included) blocks re-escalation. Off by default.
Experimental. Built-in kernel-level Zygisk injector with ZN module compatibility.
Enabling this option will allow KernelSU to restore any modified files by the modules for this app
New version %s is available, click to upgrade
Failed to update SELinux rules for %s
Manage local and online template of App Profile
Cannot find local template to export!
Automatically check for updates when opening the app
Check for signed nightly manager builds when the app opens. Disabled by default.
CI build %1$d is newer than the installed build (%2$d).
New CI build %1$d is available. Tap to download.
Failed to check for CI updates. Tap to retry.
CI update installed. Reopen the app to use it.
Automatically use Magica to bootstrap late-load when Permissive SELinux is detected at boot. Requires granting autostart permission to this app.
Can be used to debug WebUI. Please enable only when needed
Your device will be **FORCED** to boot to the current inactive slot after a reboot!\nOnly use this option after OTA is done.\nContinue?
Trigger late-load from the install page on a permissive device. If no root shell is available yet, Magica bootstrap will be used instead.
This action is only available while SELinux is permissive.
Always allow adb shell to call su. Do not enable this unless absolutely necessary.
Force enable USB debugging and disable adb authentication. Do not enable this unless absolutely necessary.
Make sure the selected boot image is an unmodified stock image.
Uninstalling KernelSU (root and all modules) completely and permanently
Restore the stock factory image (if a backup exists), usually used before OTA; if you need to uninstall KernelSU, please use "Uninstall permanently"
Launcher does not support pinned shortcuts
Please allow creating home screen shortcuts for this app in MIUI settings, then retry
Please allow creating home screen shortcuts for this app in system settings, then retry
Please check launcher permissions and allow creating home screen shortcuts, then retry
This operation will overwrite all existing modules. Continue?
This operation will overwrite the current allowlist. Continue?
Choose a new image while keeping card opacity and dim settings
Granting superuser to %s is not allowed
Disable the ability of any app to gain root privileges via the ⁠su command (existing root processes won\'t be affected)
Disable kernel-level umount behavior controlled by KernelSU
Block privilege elevation outside the KernelSU domain and unauthorized app UID downgrades
Hide KernelSU SELinux policy changes from app-facing probes
Kernel does not support this feature
This feature is managed by a module
Sure you want to install the following %1$d modules? \n\n%2$s
Hides unnecessary cards when turned on
Hides Red dot about the number of super users and modules on the navigation bar page
Hides the Seccomp status info on the home screen
Hide Zygisk implementation information on the home page
Hide Meta Module implementation information on the home page
Hide link card information on the home page
Hide folder name,size,metamodule notice labels in module cards
YukiSU is an extremely aggressive and independent KSU branch, but we still thank official KernelSU, SukiSU, Magisk, APatch and others for their contributions.
Unlock Status Hide enabled, reboot to apply
Failed to change Unlock Status Hide setting
Select the WebUI engine to use
Force the use of WebUI X
Inject a debug console into WebUI X to make debugging easier. Requires web debugging to be on
Adjust the screen display density for the current application only
Are you sure you want to change the application DPI from %1$d to %2$d?
Application needs to be restarted to apply the new DPI settings, does not affect the system status bar or other applications
DPI has been set to %1$d, effective after restarting the application
%d Failed to install a new module
This module want to mount /system, meta module will handle that. Otherwise, it might not work
Display additional module information like update JSON URLs
Enable or disable the clean mode displayed by the YukiSU kernel version
YukiZygisk is enabled; this module is disabled to prevent conflicts
You are already using YukiSU. Why not use the built-in version instead? :(
Unsupported file format. Only Magisk/KernelSU module packages are supported.
Are you sure you want to clear the selected log file? This action cannot be undone
Are you sure you want to delete the selected historical log file? This action cannot be undone
Search by UID, command, or details…
Too many logs, showing only the latest %1$d entries
The current manager is incompatible with this kernel! Please upgrade the kernel to version %2$d or higher (currently %1$d)
A reboot is required for changes to take effect. The system will apply the new configuration on the next boot
Are you sure you want to delete the path %s?
Path added, will take effect after reboot
Path removed, will take effect after reboot
Are you sure you want to clear all custom paths? (default paths will be preserved)
Enter your SuperKey to authenticate as manager
Set a SuperKey for APatch-style authentication. Leave empty to use signature-based auth.
When enabled, the manager will not save your SuperKey. You will need to enter it manually after every reboot.
After boot, authenticate the saved SuperKey with the kernel once. A failed attempt is not retried. Requires granting autostart permission to this app.
Do you really want to clear the stored SuperKey?
Disable APK signature verification. Manager will only be recognized through SuperKey authentication.
Inactive slot logical partitions may not have device nodes. Click to create them.
DANGEROUS partition! Improper operation may brick device
Flashing partition may cause device unable to boot!\n\nAre you sure you want to flash %s partition?

⚠️ Warning: %1$s is a dangerous partition!

Flashing this partition may cause:
• Device unable to boot
• Loss of important data
• Network/Bluetooth malfunction

Please make sure you know what you are doing!

Are you sure you want to flash %2$s partition?
Partition images are saved here. Edit the path before backing up.
This folder provider cannot be used for raw partition backups. Choose a local storage folder or enter a filesystem path.
Backup failed, please check logcat logs
Flash failed, please check logcat logs
Batch backup complete! Total %1$d partitions\nSaved in: %2$s
Setting saved, reboot to take effect
Sync the bundled ksud binary to /data/adb/ksud on app cold-start. Off by default; otherwise use the manual button on the home page.
An app is requesting superuser (su) access.
Show a visible su to apps and ask for authorization on first use, instead of su silently failing for apps that are not in the allowlist.
Zygote crashed repeatedly; safe mode stopped injection for this boot.
Safe mode enabled; some targets skipped.
No modules are available from enabled sources
Remove %1$s? Installed modules will keep their source history.
Modules downloaded from custom repositories are not verified by YukiSU. Confirm that you trust the source and accept the consequences before installing.
This module is no longer available from the selected source.
The repository declares %1$s (%2$d), but no matching artifact exists. YukiSU will not guess which historical version is current.
Other sources with the same module ID
This module is currently bound to another source. Continuing will explicitly switch its source.
Use this source for the installed module
MMRL’s central directory currently lists %1$d recognized repositories. None are added or enabled by default; explicitly add only the repositories you want as sources.
